title: campanula gracilis var. littoralis
intro: Campanula gracilis var. littoralis, also known as the coastal bellflower, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Campanulaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Campanula gracilis var. littoralis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit immunomodulatory effects. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing or decocting its leaves and flowers, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, and topical creams.
